commands.review_summary:
Adds a new draft RFC proposing an opt-in SQLite snapshot plugin with snapshot/restore commands, core SQLite-safe primitives, provider hooks, and future failover groundwork.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR adds RFC text and does not report a runtime bug. I checked the diff, current RFC set, related storage reports, and repository RFC lifecycle instead of running a behavior reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because accepting this RFC would set storage durability, plugin boundary, and backup/restore product direction rather than perform a narrow automated repair.

target.title: RFC 0013: SQLite State Snapshot Plugin
target.body:
## Summary

- Proposes RFC 0013, SQLite State Snapshot Plugin.
- Frames the pain points first: live SQLite files are hard to copy safely, backup is only useful when restore is verified, and failover needs a known-good state artifact.
- Keeps SQLite as the hot local runtime database and makes snapshot/restore an opt-in plugin extension.
- Allows the plugin to expose `openclaw snapshot ...` and extend the existing `openclaw backup` surface with snapshot/restore commands.
- Avoids choosing PostgreSQL or any replacement backend in this RFC.

## Scope

This is an RFC only. It does not change OpenClaw runtime behavior, add storage configuration, or implement backup/restore commands.

## Validation

- Draft follows `rfcs/0000-template.md` shape.
- Related issue context checked: openclaw/openclaw#90370, #94675, and #88838.
- Current OpenClaw CLI/docs surface checked: `openclaw backup create` and `openclaw backup verify` exist; no restore/snapshot/failover command currently owns this workflow.

## RFC Lifecycle

- Status: draft
- Implementation issue: not created
- Maintainer discussion thread: pending
